skill.name: agentspace
skill.description: Share a user-specified local folder or file through Agentspace so teammates can view, comment on, or edit generated artifacts in a browser.
skill.summary: Create a browser-accessible Agentspace link for a chosen local file or folder.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: The static weak-crypto findings are false positives from ordinary description fields, and the pipe-to-shell finding is a false positive because the skill says not to use curl piped to a shell. Real risk remains because the skill directs agents to run a third-party CLI or npm package and upload a user-selected path to agentspace.so, sometimes with edit permission.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Workspace Upload of Local Files
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run a share command on a user-specified path and upload that path to agentspace.so. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it can expose sensitive project files if the selected path is too broad or chosen without clear user confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation explicitly states that the named path is uploaded and that traffic goes to agentspace.so. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the risk is data exposure rather than confirmed malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: External CLI and Latest npm Package Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill asks agents to use ascli directly, install @agentspace-so/ascli globally, or execute the latest package with npx. Running a third-party package introduces supply-chain and command-execution risk even when the command is expected for this tool.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command execution paths are direct and intentional, including latest-version npm usage. No malicious arguments are shown, so the finding is an elevated operational risk rather than confirmed malware.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern False Positives in Documentation
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography alerts point to descriptive YAML and Markdown text, not cryptographic code. The pipe-to-shell alert appears in a safety instruction that explicitly says not to use curl piped to bash.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are documentation text and do not implement cryptography or shell piping. The shell-pipe phrase is a prohibition, not an instruction to execute it.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Sharing Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The share workflow sends a named local path to an external hosted workspace and returns a public or shareable browser URL. Users should confirm the exact path and permission level before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command and upload behavior are explicitly documented. The safety impact depends on what path the user authorizes.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote Package Execution via npx
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow permits running @agentspace-so/ascli with npx at the latest version. This can execute package code fetched from the npm registry.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx command is clearly present and uses a latest package reference. This is common for CLI tools but remains a supply-chain risk.

content.user_title: Share Agent Workspaces in a Browser
content.value_statement: Agent work often lives in local folders that teammates cannot inspect while it changes. This skill creates an Agentspace link for a chosen path so others can view, comment on, or edit the workspace.
actual_capabilities: Shares a user-specified local file or folder through the Agentspace CLI.
Returns the share URL printed by the CLI without inventing links.
Supports edit access by default and view-only access when requested.
Uses npx or a global ascli install when the CLI is available.
Provides command variants for status checks and monorepo-local execution.
limitations: It requires Node.js, npm, or an installed ascli command.
It uploads the selected path to agentspace.so, so sensitive paths need review first.
Anonymous workspaces are described as lasting 24 hours unless claimed.
It does not generate or validate share URLs without CLI output.

use_cases.title: Review generated artifacts
use_cases.description: Share logs, reports, screenshots, or generated code with a reviewer while work is still changing.

use_cases.title: Hand off an active workspace
use_cases.description: Give a teammate browser access to the exact folder the agent is producing for follow-up edits or comments.

use_cases.title: Inspect agent output remotely
use_cases.description: Open a local agent output folder from another device without packaging files or setting up a separate sync tool.

prompt_templates.title: Share one folder
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a clear target path.
prompt_templates.prompt: Share the folder ./agent-output with Agentspace and give me the link.

prompt_templates.title: Create a view-only link
prompt_templates.scenario: Safer review when the recipient should not edit files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Share ./reports as view-only through Agentspace and return only the URL printed by the CLI.

prompt_templates.title: Confirm an ambiguous target
prompt_templates.scenario: The user asks to share this folder but the exact path is unclear.
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to share this workspace. Confirm the exact folder before using Agentspace, then share it with edit access.

prompt_templates.title: Use without global install
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for machines where ascli is not installed globally.
prompt_templates.prompt: If ascli is not installed, use npx @agentspace-so/ascli@latest to share ./build-review with view-only access.

output_examples.output: The assistant confirms the path, runs the Agentspace share command, and returns the exact workspace URL printed by the CLI.

output_examples.input: Give my designer a read-only link to ./screenshots.
output_examples.output: The assistant uses view-only permission and replies with the generated Agentspace link for the screenshots folder.
The assistant asks which exact folder should be shared before running any upload command.

best_practices: Confirm the exact file or folder before sharing anything.
Use view-only permission unless collaborators need browser editing.
Review the selected path for secrets, credentials, and private data before upload.
anti_patterns: Do not default to the current directory when the user did not name a path.
Do not invent Agentspace URLs or claim links that the CLI did not print.
Do not pipe remote installation scripts into a shell.

faq.question: What does this skill share?
faq.answer: It shares the local file or folder that the user explicitly names.

faq.question: Does it upload data outside the selected path?
faq.answer: The skill instructions say only the named path should be uploaded.

faq.question: Can recipients edit the shared workspace?
faq.answer: Yes, edit permission is supported. Use view-only permission when edits are not needed.

faq.question: Does it require an Agentspace account?
faq.answer: The skill description says anonymous workspaces can be opened without signup for 24 hours.

faq.question: What tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: The main concern is uploading local files to a third-party workspace, especially if the selected path contains sensitive data.
